serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Testi Kebab Chicken with Mandi rice is approximately 350 g, comprising the clay-jug-cooked chicken portion (~150 g) and accompanying Mandi rice (~200 g).

nutrient_source.title: Testi Kebab Chicken — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00010357)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo canonical record G00010357 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #05064 (chicken with vegetables, cooked) as HPB record does not separately report saturated fat; estimated ~7.5 g/100 g based on fat composition of chicken with butter preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Testi Kebab Chicken. The dish has very low carbohydrate content (5.24 g/100 g), primarily from vegetables (tomato, bell pepper, onion). GI estimated at 45 (low) by analogy with mixed cooked vegetable and chicken dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 150 g chicken serving = (45 × 5.24 × 1.5) / 100 ≈ 3.5 g; blended serving GL reported as approximately 2.5 g (low) reflecting the low carbohydrate density of the chicken component.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper limit for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Testi Kebab Chicken due to its very high sodium content of 1080 mg/100 g; a single 150 g chicken portion delivers approximately 1620 mg sodium, representing 70–81% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low sugar (2.85 g/100 g) are favourable, but high fat (26.4 g/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g) may impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate and sugar content of Testi Kebab Chicken produces minimal glycaemic impact (estimated GI 45, GL ~3.5 per 150 g serving), making it acceptable for blood glucose management; however, the high total fat and saturated fat content should be moderated to avoid worsening insulin resistance, and high-GI accompaniments such as white rice should be replaced with lower-GI alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1080 mg/100 g), high total fat (26.4 g/100 g), and estimated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g) from butter and chicken skin collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Testi Kebab Chicken is not recommended for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its combination of very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), high total fat (26.4 g/100 g), and estimated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g); to reduce cardiovascular risk, butter should be replaced with olive oil, chicken skin removed, and salt substantially reduced.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; sodium at 1080 mg/100 g worsens RA-related tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in its standard preparation due to high saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g) and very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), both of which promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; removing chicken skin, substituting butter with olive oil, and reducing salt can bring the dish within safer parameters.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Testi Kebab Chicken is a Middle Eastern chicken dish prepared using the traditional Turkish testi (clay jug) cooking method, in which seasoned chicken pieces are placed inside a sealed clay jug together with tomatoes, bell peppers, onions, garlic, butter, and spices including thyme, cumin, and paprika. The sealed jug is slow-cooked over heat, trapping steam and allowing the ingredients to braise in their own juices, producing exceptionally tender, moist chicken with a rich, aromatic flavour profile. The clay vessel imparts a subtle earthy character to the dish. In Singapore, it is typically served with Mandi rice, a fragrant long-grain rice cooked in spiced broth. Nutritionally, the dish is high in fat (26.4 g/100 g) and very high in sodium (1080 mg/100 g), with moderate protein (10.7 g/100 g) and low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g). The high sodium content makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The low carbohydrate content is favourable for glycaemic management, but the high saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g estimated) is a concern for insulin sensitivity and cardiovascular health. Potassium (387 mg/100 g) and calcium (118 mg/100 g) are notable micronutrients. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore restaurant contexts.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo canonical record G00010357 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogue and documented. GI estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database for low-carbohydrate mixed chicken dishes. All mandatory multilingual names provided for Singapore-served Middle Eastern dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, vitamin profile, and moisture data for this traditional clay-jug chicken preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database (G00010357) or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Saturated fat_g estimated at 7.5 g/100 g based on USDA SR Legacy #05064 (chicken with vegetables, cooked with butter) as the closest approved-source analogue; this is an estimation and should be verified against laboratory analysis. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as confirmed by HPB source G00010357 — the dish preparation does not retain significant fibre from the vegetable components after slow braising in the sealed clay jug. The dish is served in Singapore restaurants and is tagged SG in the source record. The alias '44. Testi Kebab Chicken' from the input (menu numbering prefix) is preserved in the flat aliases array for retrieval completeness. Ontology match_type values corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 entries, conforming to the allowed set ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by the validator.
